assertions.description: CSS text-overflow: ellipsis is supported as the baseline declaration that this feature modifies.
CSS text-overflow: clip is supported — the value used during user interaction.
Elements honour the contenteditable attribute, the surface this feature reacts to.
window.getSelection() is exposed — needed to trigger the caret-interaction path.
Focusing an editable element with text-overflow: ellipsis does not throw and leaves text-overflow set; the user-agent transparently switches to clip while focused.
assertions.test: (()=>{const d=document.createElement('div');d.style.cssText='width:50px;white-space:nowrap;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis';d.contentEditable='true';d.textContent='this is a long string of text';document.body.appendChild(d);d.focus();const ok=getComputedStyle(d).textOverflow!=='';d.remove();return ok;})()

assertions.description: <input> form controls accept text-overflow: ellipsis (the existing behaviour this feature generalises).
